audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Rachel/Leah rivalry, Bilhah/Zilpah birth naming wordplays, mandrakes, Joseph naming, Jacob/Laban wage negotiation, and flock-breeding narrative.

verses.restored: Rachel saw that she had borne Jacob no children, and Rachel envied her sister. She said to Jacob, Give me children, or I am dead.

familiar.text: And when Rachel saw that she bare Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister...

notes.title: Give me children
notes.body: Rachel’s anguish echoes the recurring barrenness theme, now inside a divided household.

verses.restored: Jacob’s anger burned against Rachel, and he said, Am I in the place of the Divine, who has withheld from you fruit of the womb?

familiar.text: And Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel...

verses.restored: She said, Look, my slave-woman Bilhah. Go in to her, and she shall bear on my knees, so that I too may be built up through her.

familiar.text: And she said, Behold my maid Bilhah, go in unto her...

notes.title: Built up through her
notes.body: Rachel repeats the household-surrogacy logic previously seen with Sarah and Hagar.

verses.restored: She gave him Bilhah her slave-woman as a woman, and Jacob went in to her.

familiar.text: And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid to wife...

verses.restored: Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.

familiar.text: And Bilhah conceived, and bare Jacob a son.

verses.restored: Rachel said, The Divine has judged me, and has also heard my voice and given me a son. Therefore she called his name Dan.

familiar.text: And Rachel said, God hath judged me...

verses.restored: Bilhah, Rachel’s slave-woman, conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.

familiar.text: And Bilhah Rachel's maid conceived again...

verses.restored: Rachel said, With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed. She called his name Naphtali.

familiar.text: And Rachel said, With great wrestlings have I wrestled with my sister...

verses.restored: Leah saw that she had stopped bearing, so she took Zilpah her slave-woman and gave her to Jacob as a woman.

familiar.text: When Leah saw that she had left bearing, she took Zilpah...

verses.restored: Zilpah, Leah’s slave-woman, bore Jacob a son.

familiar.text: And Zilpah Leah's maid bare Jacob a son.

verses.restored: Leah said, Fortune has come! She called his name Gad.

familiar.text: And Leah said, A troop cometh: and she called his name Gad.

notes.title: Gad
notes.body: The name is associated with fortune/troop; the wording is textually and lexically debated.

verses.restored: Zilpah, Leah’s slave-woman, bore Jacob a second son.

familiar.text: And Zilpah Leah's maid bare Jacob a second son.

verses.restored: Leah said, In my happiness, for daughters will call me happy. She called his name Asher.

familiar.text: And Leah said, Happy am I, for the daughters will call me blessed...

verses.restored: Reuben went in the days of wheat harvest and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them to Leah his mother. Rachel said to Leah, Please give me some of your son’s mandrakes.

familiar.text: And Reuben went in the days of wheat harvest, and found mandrakes...

notes.title: Mandrakes
notes.body: Dudaim were associated with fertility/sexual desire in the ancient world.

verses.restored: She said to her, Is it a small thing that you have taken my man? Would you also take my son’s mandrakes? Rachel said, Therefore he shall lie with you tonight in exchange for your son’s mandrakes.

familiar.text: And she said unto her, Is it a small matter that thou hast taken my husband?...

verses.restored: Jacob came from the field in the evening, and Leah went out to meet him and said, You must come in to me, for I have surely hired you with my son’s mandrakes. So he lay with her that night.

familiar.text: And Jacob came out of the field in the evening, and Leah went out to meet him...

verses.restored: The Divine listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Jacob a fifth son.

familiar.text: And God hearkened unto Leah, and she conceived...

verses.restored: Leah said, The Divine has given me my wages, because I gave my slave-woman to my man. She called his name Issachar.

familiar.text: And Leah said, God hath given me my hire...

verses.restored: Leah conceived again and bore Jacob a sixth son.

familiar.text: And Leah conceived again, and bare Jacob the sixth son.

verses.restored: Leah said, The Divine has endowed me with a good endowment; this time my man will honor/dwell with me, because I have borne him six sons. She called his name Zebulun.

familiar.text: And Leah said, God hath endued me with a good dowry...

notes.title: Zebulun
notes.body: The name is linked with endowment/honor/dwelling wordplay.

verses.restored: Afterward she bore a daughter and called her name Dinah.

familiar.text: And afterwards she bare a daughter, and called her name Dinah.

verses.restored: The Divine remembered Rachel, and the Divine listened to her and opened her womb.

familiar.text: And God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her, and opened her womb.

notes.title: Remembered Rachel
notes.body: Divine remembering means covenantal attention and action.

verses.restored: She conceived and bore a son, and said, The Divine has gathered away my disgrace.

familiar.text: And she conceived, and bare a son; and said, God hath taken away my reproach:

verses.restored: She called his name Joseph, saying, May the Eternal add to me another son.

familiar.text: And she called his name Joseph; and said, The LORD shall add to me another son.

notes.title: Joseph
notes.body: Joseph is linked with gathering away and adding.

verses.restored: After Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Send me away, so I may go to my place and to my land.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when Rachel had born Joseph, that Jacob said unto Laban...

verses.restored: Give me my women and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go, for you know my service that I have served you.

familiar.text: Give me my wives and my children, for whom I have served thee...

verses.restored: Laban said to him, If now I have found favor in your eyes, I have divined that the Eternal has blessed me because of you.

familiar.text: And Laban said unto him, I pray thee, if I have found favour...

notes.title: Divined
notes.body: Nichashti can mean I have learned by divination/observation. Laban attributes his prosperity to Jacob.

verses.restored: He said, Name your wages to me, and I will give it.

familiar.text: And he said, Appoint me thy wages, and I will give it.

verses.restored: He said to him, You know how I have served you and how your livestock has been with me.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, Thou knowest how I have served thee...

verses.restored: For the little you had before me has burst forth into abundance, and the Eternal has blessed you at my foot. Now when shall I also provide for my own house?

familiar.text: For it was little which thou hadst before I came, and it is now increased...

verses.restored: He said, What shall I give you? Jacob said, You shall not give me anything. If you will do this thing for me, I will return, pasture your flock, and keep it.

familiar.text: And he said, What shall I give thee? And Jacob said, Thou shalt not give me any thing...

verses.restored: Let me pass through all your flock today, removing from there every speckled and spotted sheep, every dark one among the lambs, and the spotted and speckled among the goats; these shall be my wages.

familiar.text: I will pass through all thy flock to day, removing from thence all the speckled...

verses.restored: My righteousness will answer for me in a later day, when you come concerning my wages before you: every one that is not speckled or spotted among the goats, or dark among the lambs, shall be counted stolen with me.

familiar.text: So shall my righteousness answer for me in time to come...

verses.restored: Laban said, Look, let it be according to your word.

familiar.text: And Laban said, Behold, I would it might be according to thy word.

verses.restored: That day he removed the striped and spotted male goats, all the speckled and spotted female goats, every one that had white in it, and every dark one among the lambs, and put them in the hand of his sons.

familiar.text: And he removed that day the he goats that were ringstraked and spotted...

verses.restored: He set a distance of three days’ journey between himself and Jacob, and Jacob pastured the rest of Laban’s flock.

familiar.text: And he set three days' journey betwixt himself and Jacob...

verses.restored: Jacob took fresh rods of poplar, almond, and plane tree, peeled white streaks in them, exposing the white on the rods.

familiar.text: And Jacob took him rods of green poplar, and of the hazel and chesnut tree...

notes.title: Rods and streaks
notes.body: The flock-breeding method reflects ancient observation/practice, not modern genetics.

verses.restored: He set the rods he had peeled in front of the flocks, in the troughs, in the watering places where the flocks came to drink, and they bred when they came to drink.

familiar.text: And he set the rods which he had pilled before the flocks...

verses.restored: The flocks bred by the rods, and the flocks brought forth striped, speckled, and spotted.

familiar.text: And the flocks conceived before the rods, and brought forth cattle ringstraked...

verses.restored: Jacob separated the lambs and set the faces of the flock toward the striped and all the dark in Laban’s flock. He put his own droves apart and did not put them with Laban’s flock.

familiar.text: And Jacob did separate the lambs, and set the faces of the flocks...

verses.restored: Whenever the strong of the flock were breeding, Jacob put the rods before the eyes of the flock in the troughs, so that they might breed among the rods.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, whensoever the stronger cattle did conceive...

verses.restored: But when the flock was weak, he did not put them in. So the weak were Laban’s, and the strong were Jacob’s.

familiar.text: But when the cattle were feeble, he put them not in...

verses.restored: The man broke forth exceedingly, and he had many flocks, female servants, male servants, camels, and donkeys.

familiar.text: And the man increased exceedingly, and had much cattle...
